questions.original_question: Kershaw Health Limited与Lotus Technology达成了什么协议？
questions.answer:
路特斯科技与Kershaw Health Limited于2024年11月7日达成了一项债券认购协议。根据该协议，Kershaw Health Limited同意认购并购买路特斯科技发行的2029年到期的优先债券，债券本金为5亿美元，认购价格为债券本金的100%。该交易截至2025年4月30日尚未完成。

以上信息基于2025年4月30日之前的可用知识。
rewritten_question: What agreement has Kershaw Health Limited reached with Lotus Technology?

rag_info.chunk_content: On November 7, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into a bond subscription agreement (the “Bond Subscription Agreement”) with Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales (the “Subscriber”), pursuant to which the Subscriber agrees to subscribe for and purchase, and Lotus Technology hereby agrees to issue and sell to the Subscriber, a senior bond due 2029 (the “Bond”), with the principal amount of US$500,000,000, at a subscription price of 100% of the principal amount of the Bond. The Subscriber shall pay or cause to be paid the subscription price in the amount of US$500,000,000 to Lotus Technology within thirty (30) days after the issue date of the Bond. Pursuant to the Bond Subscription Agreement, prior to the purchase price of the Bonds being paid in full by the Subscriber, Lotus Technology may at its option repurchase the Bond, in whole but not in part, at a repurchase price equal to the aggregate principal amount of the Bond paid by the Subscriber to the repurchase date (if any).

rag_info.chunk_content: Bond Subscription Agreement, dated November 7, 2024, by and between Lotus Technology Inc. and Kershaw Health Limited, Form of Global Certificate.

rag_info.sub_query: What agreement has Kershaw Health Limited reached with Lotus Technology?

rag_info.chunk_content: THIS SUBSCRIPTION AGREEMENT (this “Agreement”), dated November 7, 2024, is entered into by and between (i) Lotus Technology Inc., an exempted company incorporated in the Cayman Islands (the “Company”), and Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales (the “Subscriber”). Each of the Company and the Subscriber is referred to herein individually as a “Party” and collectively as the “Parties.”
rag_info.sub_query: What agreement has Kershaw Health Limited reached with Lotus Technology?

rag_info.chunk_content: This Bond was issued under a Subscription Agreement, dated as of [●], 2024 between Lotus Technology Inc., an exempted company incorporated in the Cayman Islands (the “Issuer”) with its principal place of business at No. 800 Century Avenue, Pudong District, Shanghai, People’s Republic of China, and Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales (the “Bondholder”). Solely between the Issuer and the Bondholder, the terms of the Bond include those stated in the Subscription Agreement. The Bond is subject to all such terms, and holders are referred to the Subscription Agreement for a statement of all such terms. To the extent permitted by applicable law, in the event of any inconsistency between the terms of this Bond and the terms of the Subscription Agreement, the terms of the Subscription Agreement will control. For the avoidance of doubt, the Agents will not be bound by or have notice of any of the terms and provisions of the Subscription Agreement.
rag_info.sub_query: What agreement has Kershaw Health Limited reached with Lotus Technology?

rag_info.chunk_content: As consideration for Westwood’s irrevocable commitment to purchase ADSs upon the terms of and subject to satisfaction of the conditions set forth in the ELOC Purchase Agreement, Lotus Technology Corporation issued 700,000 Ordinary Shares to Westwood on September 19, 2024. In September 2024, Lotus Technology Corporation entered into a share sale and transfer agreement with Geely UK Limited, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Corporation sold 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH, or LTIC, a company incorporated in Germany and a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Corporation, for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000. Immediately upon completion of the transaction, Geely UK Limited beneficially owned 80% of LTIC’s share capital. On November 7, 2024, Lotus Technology Corporation entered into a bond subscription agreement, or the Bond Subscription Agreement, with Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales, or the Subscriber, pursuant to which the Subscriber agrees to subscribe for and purchase, and Lotus Technology Corporation agrees to issue and sell to the Subscriber, a senior bond due 2029, or the 2029 Bond, with the principal amount of US$500,000,000, at a subscription price of 100% of the principal amount of the Bond. As of the date of this annual report, the transaction has not been closed yet.
rag_info.sub_query: What agreement has Kershaw Health Limited reached with Lotus Technology?

rag_info.chunk_content: In connection with the transaction, 50% equity interests of one of Lotus Technology's subsidiaries will be pledged to Geely HK. On November 7, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into the Bond Subscription Agreement with Kershaw Health Limited, the Subscriber, pursuant to which the Subscriber agrees to subscribe for and purchase, and Lotus Technology agrees to issue and sell to the Subscriber, a senior bond due 2029, or the 2029 Bond, with the principal amount of US$500,000,000, at a subscription price of 100% of the principal amount of the Bond. As of the date of this annual report, the transaction has not been closed yet. In September 2024, Lotus Technology Investment Limited (LTIL) entered into a share sale and transfer agreement with Geely UK Limited, pursuant to which LTIL sold 80% equity in Lotus Technology International Company (LTIC) for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000. Immediately upon completion of the transaction, Geely UK Limited beneficially owned 80% of LTIC’s share capital. On September 29, 2024, Lotus Technology's subsidiary, Hangzhou Kingway Technology Co., Ltd., or Kingway, entered into an exchangeable note agreement with an investor. Pursuant to the agreement, Kingway is entitled to issue exchangeable notes, or the Kingway Exchangeable Notes, of RMB600,000,000 to obtain financing from the investor. The repayments of the Kingway Exchangeable Notes were guaranteed by the ultimate shareholders of Kingway in mainland China, i.e., Wuhan Lotus Technology Co., Ltd., a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology.
rag_info.sub_query: What agreement has Kershaw Health Limited reached with Lotus Technology?

rag_info.chunk_content: As previously announced, on January 31, 2023, LCAA, Lotus Technology Inc., an exempted company limited by shares incorporated under the laws of the Cayman Islands (the “Company” or “Lotus Tech”), Lotus Temp Limited, an exempted company limited by shares incorporated under the laws of the Cayman Islands and a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Tech (“Merger Sub 1”), and Lotus EV Limited, an exempted company limited by shares incorporated under the laws of the Cayman Islands and a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Tech (“Merger Sub 2”) entered into the Agreement and Plan of Merger (as amended and restated by the First Amended and Restated Agreement and Plan of Merger, dated as of October 11, 2023 and as may be further amended, supplemented or otherwise modified from time to time, the “Merger Agreement”), pursuant to which, among other things, (i) Merger Sub 1 will merge with and into LCAA (the “First Merger”), with LCAA surviving the First Merger as a wholly owned subsidiary of Lotus Tech (the surviving entity of the First Merger, “Surviving Entity 1”), and (ii) immediately following the consummation of the First Merger, Surviving Entity 1 will merge with and into Merger Sub 2 (the “Second Merger”, and together with the First Merger, collectively, the “Mergers”), with Merger Sub 2 surviving the Second Merger as a wholly owned subsidiary of Lotus Tech (the transactions contemplated by the Merger Agreement, including the Mergers, collectively, the “Business Combination”).
rag_info.sub_query: What agreement has Kershaw Health Limited reached with Lotus Technology?

rag_info.chunk_content: Concurrently with the execution of the Purchase Agreement, Lotus Technology entered into a Registration Rights Agreement with Westwood pursuant to which Lotus Technology agreed to file and have filed a registration statement to register the resale by Westwood of ADSs that may be issued to Westwood by Lotus Technology under the ELOC Purchase Agreement. Each issuance by Lotus Technology under the ELOC Purchase Agreement is subject to a maximum amount equal to 50% of the average daily trading volume in the ADSs on Nasdaq during the five trading days immediately preceding the day Westwood receives a valid purchase notice and subject to a US$100 million cap. The purchase price of ADSs that Lotus Technology may direct Westwood to purchase will be equal to 97% of the lowest daily volume-weighted average price during the three consecutive trading days beginning on the date that a purchase notice is delivered by Lotus Technology to Westwood. As consideration for Westwood’s irrevocable commitment to purchase ADSs upon the terms of and subject to satisfaction of the conditions set forth in the ELOC Purchase Agreement, Lotus Technology issued 700,000 Ordinary Shares to Westwood on September 19, 2024. In September 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. entered into a share sale and transfer agreement with Geely UK Limited, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Inc. sold 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH, or LTIC, a company incorporated in Germany and a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Inc., for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000.
rag_info.sub_query: What agreement has Kershaw Health Limited reached with Lotus Technology?

rag_info.chunk_content: Immediately upon completion of the transaction, Geely UK Limited beneficially owned 80% of LTIC’s share capital. On November 7, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into a bond subscription agreement, or the Bond Subscription Agreement, with Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales, or the Subscriber, pursuant to which the Subscriber agrees to subscribe for and purchase, and Lotus Technology agrees to issue and sell to the Subscriber, a senior bond due 2029, or the 2029 Bond, with the principal amount of US$500,000,000, at a subscription price of 100% of the principal amount of the Bond. As of the date of this prospectus, the transaction has not been closed yet. On November 29, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into a share buyback agreement, or the Share Buyback Agreement, with Meritz, pursuant to which Meritz agrees to sell and surrender, and Lotus Technology agrees to repurchase, 17,500,000 ADSs, or the Repurchased ADSs. Ordinary Shares underlying the Repurchased ADSs constitute a part of the 50,000,000 Ordinary Shares, or the Meritz Shares, issued by Lotus Technology to Meritz concurrently with the closing of the Business Combination pursuant to the Meritz Subscription Agreement. The total consideration payable by Lotus Technology for the buyback equals the amount that provides Meritz with a 12.5% internal rate of return, calculated for the period between the Closing Date and the date of the closing of the buyback (referred to as the “Buyback Closing” and the date of the Buyback Closing, the “Buyback Closing Date”) on US$175 million.

metadata.title_summary:
title: EXHIBIT INDEX 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of the Bond Subscription Agreement**

The Bond Subscription Agreement, dated November 7, 2024, is a formal legal document established between Lotus Technology Inc. and Kershaw Health Limited. This agreement delineates the terms under which Kershaw Health Limited will subscribe to bonds issued by Lotus Technology Inc. 

Key components of the agreement include the form of the Global Certificate, which specifies the structure and representation of the bonds. The document outlines critical financial metrics such as the total amount of bonds to be issued, the applicable interest rates, and the maturity dates of the bonds. Additionally, it includes specific covenants and obligations that both parties must adhere to throughout the duration of the agreement.

To ensure compliance with relevant financial regulations, the document is structured to meet all applicable regulatory and legal standards. This adherence is crucial for maintaining the integrity of the bond issuance process and safeguarding the interests of both Lotus Technology Inc. and Kershaw Health Limited.

Overall, the Bond Subscription Agreement serves as a comprehensive framework governing the subscription of bonds, encapsulating all essential financial details, regulatory compliance, and the obligations of the involved parties.

title: SUBSCRIPTION AGREEMENT 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of the Subscription Agreement**

The Subscription Agreement, dated November 7, 2024, is a legally binding document between Lotus Technology Inc., an exempted company incorporated in the Cayman Islands (hereafter referred to as the "Company"), and Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales (hereafter referred to as the "Subscriber"). The parties involved are referred to individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."

This agreement delineates the terms and conditions that govern the subscription arrangement between the Company and the Subscriber. It includes critical information regarding the rights and obligations of both Parties, the nature of the subscription, and any associated regulatory and legal stipulations that must be adhered to throughout the duration of the agreement.

The document serves to ensure clarity and mutual understanding regarding the investment and operational commitments made by both Parties, thereby establishing a framework for cooperation and compliance with applicable laws and regulations. 

In summary, the Subscription Agreement is a foundational document that formalizes the relationship between Lotus Technology Inc. and Kershaw Health Limited, detailing the essential elements of their subscription arrangement while ensuring legal and regulatory compliance.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Bond Subscription Agreement 
summary: On November 7, 2024, the Company entered into a Bond Subscription Agreement with Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales. Under this agreement, Kershaw Health Limited will subscribe for and purchase a senior bond with a principal amount of $500,000,000, which is due in 2029. The subscription price for the bond is set at 100% of the principal amount, amounting to $500,000,000. The Subscriber is obligated to pay this subscription price to the Company within 30 days following the bond's issue date.

Additionally, the Company retains the right to repurchase the Bond, in whole but not in part, at a price equal to the aggregate principal amount paid by the Subscriber up to the repurchase date, provided that the purchase price has not been fully paid. The Bond Subscription Agreement, along with the Form of Global Certificate, is included in the current report on Form 6-K as Exhibits 10.1 and 10.2, respectively. The description of the agreement is qualified by reference to these documents, ensuring compliance with relevant regulatory and legal requirements.

The Subscriber shall pay or cause to be paid the subscription price in the amount of US$500,000 to Lotus Technology within thirty (30) days after the issue date of the Bond. metadata.title_summary:
title: 20. SENIOR BOND 
summary: On November 7, 2024, the Company entered into a Bond Subscription Agreement with Kershaw Health Limited, a private entity from England and Wales. This agreement involves Kershaw Health Limited purchasing a senior bond, set to mature in 2029, with a principal amount of $500,000 at a subscription price of 100%. The payment for the bond is due within 30 days of its issuance. The Company also has the right to repurchase the bond, in full, before the Subscriber has completed the payment, for the amount equal to the principal paid up to the repurchase date. As of December 31, 2024, the bond has not yet been issued.

Ordinary Shares underlying the Repurchased ADSs constitute a part of the 50,000,000 Ordinary Shares, or the Meritz Shares, issued by Lotus Technology to Meritz concurrently with the closing of the Business Combination pursuant to the Meritz Subscription Agreement. The total consideration payable by Lotus Technology for the buyback equals the amount that provides Meritz with a 12.5% internal rate of return, calculated for the period between the Closing Date and the date of the closing of the buyback (Lotus Technology refers to such closing as the “Buyback Closing” and the date of the Buyback Closing, the “Buyback Closing Date”) on US$175 million.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Business Updates 
summary: In 2024, our company achieved significant growth, delivering 12,065 vehicles, a remarkable increase of over 70% compared to the previous year, with China contributing around 25% of global deliveries. A convertible note purchase agreement was established with Geely HK on June 24, 2024, involving the acquisition of convertible notes totaling a specified amount in U.S. dollars, which Geely HK is raising through a private placement of RMB800 million. These notes, maturing on June 22, 2025, carry an interest rate of SORF plus 3.35% per annum, with conversion options into Ordinary Shares or American Depositary Shares (ADSs) starting 30 trading days post-issuance.

On September 16, 2024, we entered into an ELOC Purchase Agreement with Westwood Capital Group LLC, allowing for the purchase of up to $350 million in ADSs over 36 months, with specific purchase limitations and pricing conditions. Concurrently, we issued 700,000 Ordinary Shares to Westwood as part of this agreement. In September, we also sold 800,000 shares of our German subsidiary, Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH, to Geely UK Limited for EUR 10,880,000, resulting in Geely UK acquiring an 80% stake in LTIC.

On November 7, 2024, we established a bond subscription agreement with Kershaw Health Limited for a senior bond of $500 million, which is still pending finalization. Later that month, a share buyback agreement with Meritz was initiated, involving the repurchase of 17,500,000 ADSs linked to a previous business combination, structured to ensure a 12.5% internal rate of return for Meritz. This buyback is still under discussion.

In terms of leadership changes, Mr. Teik Huat Ooi resigned as director on December 6, 2024, and Mr. Abdul Farid Bin Alias was appointed effective January 22, 2025. Finally, on February 28, 2025, we entered into another convertible note purchase agreement with Geely HK for $70 million, with similar terms to previous agreements, including a maturity date of February 28, 2027, and a pledge of 50% equity interests in one of our subsidiaries.

As of the date of this prospectus, the transaction has not been closed yet. On November 29, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into a share buyback agreement, or the Share Buyback Agreement, with Meritz, pursuant to which Meritz agrees to sell and surrender, and Lotus Technology agrees to repurchase, 17,500,000 ADSs, or the Repurchased ADSs. Ordinary Shares underlying the Repurchased ADSs constitute a part of the 50,000,000 Ordinary Shares, or the Meritz Shares, issued by Lotus Technology to Meritz concurrently with the closing of the Business Combination pursuant to the Meritz Subscription Agreement. The total consideration payable by Lotus Technology for the buyback equals the amount that provides Meritz with a 12.5% internal rate of return, calculated for the period between the Closing Date and the date of the closing of the buyback (referred to as the “Buyback Closing” and the date of the Buyback Closing, the “Buyback Closing Date”) on US$175 million.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Business Updates 
summary: In 2024, the company reported impressive preliminary results, delivering 12,065 vehicles, which represents a year-on-year growth of over 70%, with China contributing about 25% of global deliveries. A significant development occurred on June 24, 2024, when a convertible note purchase agreement was signed with Geely HK, allowing Geely to acquire convertible notes totaling a specified amount in U.S. dollars. These notes, which amount to RMB800 million, will mature on June 22, 2025, and carry an interest rate of SORF plus 3.35% per annum, with the option for Geely to convert them into Ordinary Shares or American Depositary Shares (ADSs) after 30 trading days.

On September 16, 2024, the company entered into an ELOC Purchase Agreement with Westwood Capital Group LLC, permitting Westwood to purchase up to $350 million in ADSs over a three-year period, subject to specific conditions. This agreement included a Registration Rights Agreement to facilitate the resale of the ADSs. Additionally, the company issued 700,000 Ordinary Shares to Westwood as part of the arrangement.

In September 2024, a share sale agreement was made with Geely UK Limited, resulting in the sale of 800,000 shares of the wholly-owned subsidiary, Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH, for EUR 10,880,000, granting Geely UK an 80% stake in LTIC. A bond subscription agreement with Kershaw Health Limited was also established on November 7, 2024, for a senior bond of $500 million due in 2029, although this transaction is still pending.

On November 29, 2024, a share buyback agreement with Meritz was finalized, involving the purchase of 17,500,000 ADSs, which will allow Meritz to achieve a 12.5% internal rate of return on its $175 million investment. This buyback will also release several obligations under the original subscription agreement with Meritz. 

In terms of leadership changes, Mr. Teik Huat Ooi resigned as a director on December 6, 2024, and Mr. Abdul Farid Bin Alias was appointed to the position effective January 22, 2025. Looking ahead, on February 28, 2025, another convertible note purchase agreement with Geely HK is anticipated, involving $70 million in convertible notes maturing in 2027, with similar terms regarding conversion and interest.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In connection with the transaction, 50% equity interests of one of Lotus Technology's subsidiaries will be pledged to Geely HK. On November 7, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into the Bond Subscription Agreement with Kershaw Health Limited, the Subscriber, pursuant to which the Subscriber agrees to subscribe for and purchase, and Lotus Technology agrees to issue and sell to the Subscriber, a senior bond due 2029, or the 2029 Bond, with the principal amount of US$500,000,000, at a subscription price of 100% of the principal amount of the Bond. As of the date of this annual report, the transaction has not been closed yet. In September 2024, Lotus Technology Investment Limited (LTIL) entered into a share sale and transfer agreement with Geely UK Limited, pursuant to which LTIL sold 80% equity in Lotus Technology International Company (LTIC) for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000. Immediately upon completion of the transaction, Geely UK Limited beneficially owned 80% of LTIC’s share capital. On September 29, 2024, Lotus Technology's subsidiary, Hangzhou Kingway Technology Co., Ltd., or Kingway, entered into an exchangeable note agreement with an investor. Pursuant to the agreement, Kingway is entitled to issue exchangeable notes, or the Kingway Exchangeable Notes, of RMB600,000,000 to obtain financing from the investor. The repayments of the Kingway Exchangeable Notes were guaranteed by the ultimate shareholders of Kingway in mainland China, i.e., Wuhan Lotus Technology Co., Ltd., a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Cash flows and working capital 
summary: The cash flow summary for the years 2022 to 2024 provides a detailed analysis of the organization's financial movements, emphasizing operating, investing, and financing activities. In 2024, the organization reported a net cash outflow of $848,520 from operating activities, an increase from previous years, while investing activities also saw significant outflows totaling $579,440. However, financing activities generated a substantial net inflow of $1,459,527, reflecting the organization's reliance on various financing mechanisms, including bank loans and shareholder contributions.

The cash positions at the beginning of 2024 were $427,135, a decrease from prior years, but by the end of 2024, cash and restricted cash rose to $484,937. Exchange rate fluctuations positively impacted cash balances in 2024, contrasting with negative effects in the previous two years. Overall, the net cash increase for 2024 was $57,802, a turnaround from the decrease experienced in 2023.

Several significant financing agreements were established in 2024, including a $70 million convertible bond sale to Geely HK, a $500 million senior bond subscription with Kershaw Health Limited, and an exchangeable note agreement totaling RMB 600 million by Hangzhou Kingway Technology. The organization also engaged in a share sale agreement with Geely UK Limited and secured short-term loans backed by intellectual property.

Despite these financing efforts, the organization faces challenges related to profitability and cash flow management, raising concerns about its ability to sustain operations without effective strategies for securing additional funding. The company is exploring various options, including cutting expenses and pursuing new loans or equity financing, amidst uncertainties in global financial markets.

Additionally, the organization’s revenue is primarily denominated in RMB, which may complicate capital expenses and foreign currency conversions due to regulatory requirements in mainland China. The summary underscores the importance of effective cash management and strategic planning to navigate financial challenges and ensure continued operational viability.

metadata.title_summary:
title: FORM OF REVERSE OF GLOBAL CERTIFICATE 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. 6.0% Bond due 2029 Terms and Conditions**

The LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. 6.0% Bond, maturing on [●], 2029, is structured to provide bondholders with a principal amount due at maturity and an annual interest rate of 6.0%. Interest payments will be made in arrears, commencing on [●], 2025, to those bondholders recorded as of one Clearing System Business Day prior to the Interest Payment Date. The interest calculation is based on a 360-day year.

This bond was issued under a Subscription Agreement dated [●], 2024, between Lotus Technology Inc. (the Issuer) and Kershaw Health Limited (the Bondholder). The bond's governance is further defined by a Fiscal Agency Agreement dated [●], 2024, with Deutsche Bank AG serving as the fiscal agent.

The Issuer retains the right to repurchase the bonds for cancellation prior to the Repurchase Date, which is defined as 30 days post-issue or another mutually agreed date. Such repurchase must be communicated to bondholders at least 7 Business Days before the Repurchase Date, and will occur at the principal amount.

After the full payment of the principal, the Issuer may opt for optional redemption of the bond at 100% of the principal plus any accrued interest, provided that notice is given 30 to 60 days in advance. It is important to note that bonds with a principal amount of $200,000 or less cannot be partially redeemed.

The bond is issued in registered form, with minimum denominations set at $200,000. In circumstances where the common depositary is unavailable, the Issuer will issue Definitive Certificates in exchange for the Global Certificate.

Events of Default are clearly defined and include: (a) a 30-day default in interest payments; (b) default in principal payments; (c) defaults by the Issuer or its Significant Subsidiaries on debts exceeding $100 million; (d) failure to pay judgments over $100 million; (e) initiation of voluntary or involuntary bankruptcy proceedings; and (f) involuntary proceedings that remain undismissed for 60 days.

This summary provides a comprehensive overview of the key terms, conditions, and obligations associated with the LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. 6.0% Bond due 2029, ensuring clarity on financial metrics, legal stipulations, and procedural requirements for potential investors and stakeholders.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Inc. 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of the Subscription Agreement between Lotus Technology Inc. and Kershaw Health Limited**

On November 7, 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. formalized a Subscription Agreement with Kershaw Health Limited, with the agreement being executed by Alexious Kuen Long Lee, the Director and Chief Financial Officer of Lotus Technology Inc. This agreement establishes a comprehensive framework for a bond transaction between the two entities, detailing the obligations, representations, and conditions necessary for the successful closing of the deal.

**Key Articles of the Agreement:**

1. **Article I: Definition and Interpretation**
   - This section provides essential definitions and rules of construction relevant to the interpretation of the agreement.

2. **Article II: Purchase and Sale; Closing**
   - **Section 2.01**: Specifies the terms regarding the issuance, sale, and subscription of the bond.
   - **Section 2.02**: Outlines the procedures that must be followed to close the transaction.

3. **Article III: Conditions to Closing**
   - **Section 3.01**: Lists the conditions that must be satisfied by the Subscriber prior to closing.
   - **Section 3.02**: Enumerates the conditions that must be fulfilled by Lotus Technology Inc. before the transaction can be finalized.

4. **Article IV: Representations and Warranties**
   - **Section 4.01**: Details the representations and warranties made by Lotus Technology Inc. to Kershaw Health Limited.
   - **Section 4.02**: Specifies the representations and warranties provided by Kershaw Health Limited to Lotus Technology Inc.

5. **Article V: Covenants and Further Agreement**
   - **Section 5.01**: Addresses the necessary consents and approvals required for the transaction.
   - **Section 5.02**: Discusses the payment obligations related to the subscription price.
   - **Section 5.03**: Imposes restrictions on the assignment of rights under the agreement.
   - **Section 5.04**: States that no drawdown of funds is permitted under the terms of the agreement.
   - **Section 5.05**: Grants Lotus Technology Inc. the right to cancel the agreement under specified circumstances.
   - **Section 5.06**: Addresses the allocation of expenses incurred in connection with the agreement.
   - **Section 5.07**: Outlines the procedures for settlement between the parties.
   - **Section 5.08**: Details the conditions precedent that must be met for Lotus Technology Inc.'s covenants to be enforceable.

This Subscription Agreement serves as a legally binding document that governs the relationship between Lotus Technology Inc. and Kershaw Health Limited concerning the bond transaction, ensuring that all parties are aware of their rights, obligations, and the necessary conditions for successful execution.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: As consideration for Westwood’s irrevocable commitment to purchase ADSs upon the terms of and subject to satisfaction of the conditions set forth in the ELOC Purchase Agreement, Lotus Technology Corporation issued 700,000 Ordinary Shares to Westwood on September 19, 2024. In September 2024, Lotus Technology Corporation entered into a share sale and transfer agreement with Geely UK Limited, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Corporation sold 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH, or LTIC, a company incorporated in Germany and a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Corporation, for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000. Immediately upon completion of the transaction, Geely UK Limited beneficially owned 80% of LTIC’s share capital. On November 7, 2024, Lotus Technology Corporation entered into a bond subscription agreement, or the Bond Subscription Agreement, with Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales, or the Subscriber, pursuant to which the Subscriber agrees to subscribe for and purchase, and Lotus Technology Corporation agrees to issue and sell to the Subscriber, a senior bond due 2029, or the 2029 Bond, with the principal amount of US$500,000,000, at a subscription price of 100% of the principal amount of the Bond. As of the date of this annual report, the transaction has not been closed yet.

metadata.title_summary:
title: A. History and Development of the Company 
summary: The Lotus BEV business, founded in 2018, operates through various entities in China, the UK, and Germany, including Wuhan Lotus Cars and Lotus Tech UK. A significant restructuring in 2023 eliminated the previous Variable Interest Entity (VIE) structure, streamlining operations under subsidiaries in mainland China. Key developments include the establishment of LTC in the Cayman Islands in 2021, the acquisition of equity interests in Lotus Tech UK and Lotus GmbH, and trademark licensing agreements with Group Lotus Limited. 

In March 2022, LTC executed a 10-for-1 stock split, increasing its ordinary shares significantly. The company continued its restructuring into 2023, moving away from the VIE model. In February 2024, LTC completed a business combination with LCAA, leading to the trading of its American Depositary Shares (ADSs) on Nasdaq. Subsequent financial activities included a private placement of convertible bonds with Geely HK and agreements for share purchases and bond subscriptions, indicating ongoing capital raising efforts. 

By September 2024, LTC had entered into agreements for significant share sales and bond subscriptions, including a $500 million bond agreement with Kershaw Health Limited. Future plans involve the acquisition of a controlling interest in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, which is crucial for manufacturing and consultancy services related to Lotus' sports cars and hypercars. The company’s principal executive office is located in Shanghai, China, with additional information available through its corporate website and the SEC's site.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Copies of the Bond Subscription Agreement and the Form of Global Certificate are included in this current report on Form 6-K as Exhibit 10.1 and Exhibit 10.2, respectively, and the foregoing description of the Bond Subscription Agreement and the Bond is qualified in its entirety by reference thereto.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Bond Subscription Agreement 
summary: On November 7, 2024, the Company entered into a Bond Subscription Agreement with Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales. Under this agreement, Kershaw Health Limited will subscribe for and purchase a senior bond with a principal amount of $500,000,000, which is due in 2029. The subscription price for the bond is set at 100% of the principal amount, amounting to $500,000,000. The Subscriber is obligated to pay this subscription price to the Company within 30 days following the bond's issue date.

Additionally, the Company retains the right to repurchase the Bond, in whole but not in part, at a price equal to the aggregate principal amount paid by the Subscriber up to the repurchase date, provided that the purchase price has not been fully paid. The Bond Subscription Agreement, along with the Form of Global Certificate, is included in the current report on Form 6-K as Exhibits 10.1 and 10.2, respectively. The description of the agreement is qualified by reference to these documents, ensuring compliance with relevant regulatory and legal requirements.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology’s American Depositary Shares will begin trading on the Nasdaq on February 23, 2024, under the ticker symbol “LOT.” New York and Singapore - February 20, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“L CAA”) (NASDAQ: LCAA), a special purpose acquisition company formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm, announced today that their previously proposed business combination is expected to be completed on February 22, 2024. The combined company will retain Lotus Technology Inc.'s name as “Lotus Technology Inc.” and its American Depositary Shares (ADS) will commence trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol “LOT” on February 23, 2024. The business combination was approved by L CAA shareholders at an extraordinary general meeting held on February 2, 2024. Integrating Lotus Technology’s sports car DNA with a new generation of premium lifestyle vehicles, Lotus Technology Inc. is committed to transforming the brand into an advanced, fully electric, intelligent, and sustainable luxury mobility provider before its 80th anniversary in 2028. Lotus Technology Inc. has already set this ambitious plan in motion by launching a leading product portfolio of high-performing battery electric vehicles (“BEVs”) ahead of other global luxury automakers. These BEVs include Eletre, a new breed of all-electric hyper-SUV powered by Lotus Technology’s proprietary 800-volt EPA, and Emeya, an all-electric hyper-GT among the most advanced vehicles of its kind in the world.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Tech and  L  Catterton Asia Acquisition
Corp Announce Closing of Business Combination 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc., a luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, is poised to make its debut on the Nasdaq stock exchange on February 23, 2024, trading under the ticker symbol "LOT." This significant milestone follows the completion of its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA), which is set to finalize on February 22, 2024, after receiving shareholder approval on February 2, 2024. Lotus Tech aims to transform into a fully electric and sustainable luxury mobility provider by 2028, coinciding with its 80th anniversary, and plans to achieve a 100% electric product portfolio by 2027. The company has already introduced high-performing battery electric vehicles, such as the Eletre hyper-SUV and the Emeya hyper-GT, with plans for two additional models in the next two years. Since announcing the merger, Lotus Tech has secured over $880 million in financing commitments, marking one of the largest de-SPAC transactions since 2023. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed enthusiasm for the company's growth as a public entity, while L CAA Co-CEO Chinta Bhagat highlighted their shared vision for establishing a leading luxury electric vehicle brand. To commemorate its public listing, Lotus Tech will ring the Nasdaq opening bell on February 23, an event that will be live-streamed for viewers.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: With both BEVs already on the roads and two more fully electric models planned for launch over the next two years, Lotus Technology Inc. is on track to become the first traditional luxury automotive brand to achieve a 100% electric product portfolio by 2027. Since the transaction was announced, Lotus Technology Inc. has raised more than US$880 million in pre-closing and private investment in public equity (“PIPE”) financing commitments from global investors, existing shareholders, and strategic partners, representing one of the largest amounts of additional financings raised in connection with a de-SPAC transaction since 2023. “Lotus Technology Inc. is thrilled to announce its upcoming debut on the Nasdaq as it completes its business combination with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp,” said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer of Lotus Technology Inc. “This is a pivotal moment in our journey. Lotus Technology Inc. looks forward to accelerating its growth as a listed company, leading the electric transformation of the global luxury battery electric vehicle market together with L Catterton.” “L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp's partnership with Lotus Technology Inc. has gotten stronger over time and our respect for the mission Lotus Technology Inc. is on has only increased,” said Chinta Bhagat, Co-Chief Executive Officer of L CAA. “We are excited about the road ahead as we continue building the definitive luxury electric vehicle brand together, leveraging our global consumer insights and our strategic relationship with LVMH.

New York and Singapore  
- February 22, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“LCAA”) (NASDAQ: LCAA), a special purpose acquisition company formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm, today announced the completion of their previously announced business combination (“Business Combination”). The listed company following the Business Combination is “Lotus Technology Inc.” and its American depositary shares (“ADSs”) are expected to commence trading on the Nasdaq Stock Market LLC (“Nasdaq”) under the ticker symbol “LOT” on February 23, 2024. The announcement of the completion of the Business Combination comes after L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp shareholders voted to approve the transaction on February 2, 2024. As a result of the Business Combination, L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp became a wholly owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Inc. and is expected to be delisted from the Nasdaq. Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer of Lotus Technology Inc., said: "Becoming a U.S.-listed company marks a significant milestone in Lotus Technology Inc.'s advancement into a global sustainable luxury mobility provider under the brand's Vision80 strategy.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Tech and  L  Catterton Asia Acquisition
Corp Complete Business Combination 
summary: On February 22, 2024, Lotus Technology Inc., a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, announced the successful completion of its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA), a special purpose acquisition company backed by L Catterton. Following shareholder approval on February 2, 2024, LCAA has become a wholly owned subsidiary of Lotus Tech, which will continue to operate under its name. The company's American depositary shares (ADSs) are set to begin trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol "LOT" on February 23, 2024. CEO Qingfeng Feng highlighted this listing as a significant milestone in Lotus Tech's ambition to lead in sustainable luxury mobility, while LCAA Co-CEO Chinta Bhagat expressed optimism about the growth opportunities this merger presents. To commemorate the occasion, Lotus Tech will ring the Nasdaq opening bell on February 23, 2024, with the event available for live streaming.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: I look forward to further collaboration with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp as our respected partner, joining us in contributing to a greener future for all through sustainable innovation." Chinta Bhagat, Co-Chief Executive Officer of L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, said: "L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp is very pleased to announce the successful completion of the business combination with Lotus Technology Inc., a pioneer in the electrification of luxury mobility with a world-leading product portfolio of luxury vehicles. L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp is confident that Lotus Technology Inc.’s listing on the Nasdaq will further its global expansion, and looks forward to joining Lotus Technology Inc. in the exciting growth journey ahead." Lotus Technology Inc. is expected to ring the Nasdaq opening bell in New York City on February 23, 2024, to commemorate its public listing. A live stream of the event can be viewed at https://www.nasdaq.com/marketsite/bell-ringing-ceremony.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: As previously announced, on January 31, 2023, LCAA, Lotus Technology Inc., an exempted company limited by shares incorporated under the laws of the Cayman Islands (the “Company” or “Lotus Tech”), Lotus Temp Limited, an exempted company limited by shares incorporated under the laws of the Cayman Islands and a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Tech (“Merger Sub 1”), and Lotus EV Limited, an exempted company limited by shares incorporated under the laws of the Cayman Islands and a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Tech (“Merger Sub 2”) entered into the Agreement and Plan of Merger (as amended and restated by the First Amended and Restated Agreement and Plan of Merger, dated as of October 11, 2023 and as may be further amended, supplemented or otherwise modified from time to time, the “Merger Agreement”), pursuant to which, among other things, (i) Merger Sub 1 will merge with and into LCAA (the “First Merger”), with LCAA surviving the First Merger as a wholly owned subsidiary of Lotus Tech (the surviving entity of the First Merger, “Surviving Entity 1”), and (ii) immediately following the consummation of the First Merger, Surviving Entity 1 will merge with and into Merger Sub 2 (the “Second Merger”, and together with the First Merger, collectively, the “Mergers”), with Merger Sub 2 surviving the Second Merger as a wholly owned subsidiary of Lotus Tech (the transactions contemplated by the Merger Agreement, including the Mergers, collectively, the “Business Combination”).

metadata.title_summary:
title: Item 8.01 Other Events 
summary: On January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Inc. and its subsidiaries, Lotus Temp Limited and Lotus EV Limited, entered into a Merger Agreement that outlines a two-step merger process. Initially, Lotus Temp Limited will merge with LCAA, making LCAA a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Tech. Subsequently, the newly formed entity will merge with Lotus EV Limited, which will also become a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Tech. This series of transactions, collectively known as the Business Combination, was amended on October 11, 2023.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York - July 1, 2025 - Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”) (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced that Etika Automotive Sdn Bhd (“Etika”) exercised its put option on June 30, 2025, requiring Lotus Technology Inc. to purchase 49% of the equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd (“Lotus UK”) held by Etika pursuant to the Put Option Agreement dated January 31, 2023, between the parties. Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd (“Lotus UK”) is currently owned by Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited (“Geely HK”) and Etika. As announced on April 14, 2025, Geely HK exercised a similar put option granted to Geely HK by Lotus Technology Inc. With Etika having exercised its put option, Lotus Technology Inc. is expected to acquire 100% of the equity interests of Lotus UK through non-cash transactions based on pre-agreed prices. Upon completion, Lotus Technology Inc. will gain control over Lotus UK and consolidate its financial results. The strategic transaction will enable Lotus Technology Inc. to integrate all businesses and operations under the Lotus brand. Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd controls the manufacturing operations of Lotus Technology Inc.'s sportscars and hypercars, and Lotus Engineering provides comprehensive consultancy services to many of the OEMs and Tier 1 suppliers around the world. The acquisition of 49% of the equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd is expected to be completed by 2025, subject to potential regulatory approvals.

metadata.title_summary:
title: otus Technology to Acquire $1 0 0 \%$ Equity Interest
of Lotus Advance Technologies and Integrate All Business and Operations under Lotus Brand 
summary: On July 1, 2025, Lotus Technology Inc. (Nasdaq: LOT) announced that Etika Automotive Sdn Bhd exercised its put option on June 30, 2025, compelling Lotus Tech to acquire 49% of Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd (Lotus UK). This follows Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited's earlier exercise of a put option on April 14, 2025. As a result, Lotus Tech is set to gain full control of Lotus UK through non-cash transactions at pre-agreed prices, allowing for the consolidation of its financial results and integration of operations under the Lotus brand. Lotus UK is responsible for producing Lotus sportscars and hypercars and offers consultancy services through Lotus Engineering. The acquisition is expected to be completed by 2025, subject to regulatory approvals. The agreements with Geely HK and Etika were established as part of Lotus Tech's business combination with $L$ Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, with the condition that Lotus UK and its subsidiaries sell over 5,000 vehicles in 2024, a threshold that has already been met as of December 31, 2024. In exchange for the shares of Lotus UK, Lotus Tech will issue new shares priced at $10 each, with the company's valuation based on a multiple of 1.15 times its revenue, adjusted for cash and debt, according to its 2024 audited financial report.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In conjunction with the business combination between Lotus Technology Inc. and $L$ Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, a special purpose acquisition company affiliated with $L$ Catterton, Lotus Technology Inc. and Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd entered into a Put Option Agreement with each of Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited and Etika Automotive Sdn Bhd. Pursuant to these agreements, each of Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited and Etika Automotive Sdn Bhd was granted the right to require Lotus Technology Inc. to acquire its equity interest in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd at a pre-agreed pricing method and upon satisfaction of the condition (“Put Option Exercise Condition”) that the total number of vehicles sold by Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd and its subsidiaries shall exceed 5,000 in 2024, with the exercise of such options by Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited and Etika Automotive Sdn Bhd not cross-conditioned on one another. As of December 31, 2024, the Put Option Exercise Condition had been satisfied. Note 1: Pursuant to the agreement, Lotus Technology Inc. will issue new shares valued at $10 per share in consideration of Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited and Etika Automotive Sdn Bhd’s transfer of Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd shares. Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd is valued at 1.15 multiples revenue plus cash minus debt of LGIL, based on LGIL’s audited consolidated annual financial report of 2024.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: As preliminary results, Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70%. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. On June 24, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into a convertible note purchase agreement, or the 2024 Geely CB Purchase Agreement, with Geely HK, pursuant to which Geely HK purchased from Lotus Technology convertible notes, or the 2024 Geely CBs, in two tranches in an aggregate principal amount equal to the U.S. dollar equivalent of RMB800 million through a private placement. Each note matures on June 22, 2025, and bears an interest rate per annum equal to SORF on the issue date plus 3.35%, payable on the maturity date. Subject to the terms of the applicable note, the 2024 Geely CBs may be convertible into Ordinary Shares or ADSs at the option of Geely HK starting from the 30th trading day after the applicable issue date. The initial conversion price is equal to the volume-weighted average of the last reported sale price of the ADSs over the 10 consecutive trading days immediately preceding the applicable conversion date. On September 16, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into the ELOC Purchase Agreement with Westwood Capital Group LLC, or Westwood, pursuant to which Westwood has committed to purchase, subject to certain conditions, up to US$350,000,000, or the Total Commitment, of ADSs during a commitment period of approximately 36 months.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Business Updates 
summary: In 2024, our company achieved significant growth, delivering 12,065 vehicles, a remarkable increase of over 70% compared to the previous year, with China contributing around 25% of global deliveries. A convertible note purchase agreement was established with Geely HK on June 24, 2024, involving the acquisition of convertible notes totaling a specified amount in U.S. dollars, which Geely HK is raising through a private placement of RMB800 million. These notes, maturing on June 22, 2025, carry an interest rate of SORF plus 3.35% per annum, with conversion options into Ordinary Shares or American Depositary Shares (ADSs) starting 30 trading days post-issuance.

On September 16, 2024, we entered into an ELOC Purchase Agreement with Westwood Capital Group LLC, allowing for the purchase of up to $350 million in ADSs over 36 months, with specific purchase limitations and pricing conditions. Concurrently, we issued 700,000 Ordinary Shares to Westwood as part of this agreement. In September, we also sold 800,000 shares of our German subsidiary, Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH, to Geely UK Limited for EUR 10,880,000, resulting in Geely UK acquiring an 80% stake in LTIC.

On November 7, 2024, we established a bond subscription agreement with Kershaw Health Limited for a senior bond of $500 million, which is still pending finalization. Later that month, a share buyback agreement with Meritz was initiated, involving the repurchase of 17,500,000 ADSs linked to a previous business combination, structured to ensure a 12.5% internal rate of return for Meritz. This buyback is still under discussion.

In terms of leadership changes, Mr. Teik Huat Ooi resigned as director on December 6, 2024, and Mr. Abdul Farid Bin Alias was appointed effective January 22, 2025. Finally, on February 28, 2025, we entered into another convertible note purchase agreement with Geely HK for $70 million, with similar terms to previous agreements, including a maturity date of February 28, 2027, and a pledge of 50% equity interests in one of our subsidiaries.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Concurrently with the execution of the Purchase Agreement, Lotus Technology entered into a Registration Rights Agreement with Westwood pursuant to which Lotus Technology agreed to file and have filed a registration statement to register the resale by Westwood of ADSs that may be issued to Westwood by Lotus Technology under the ELOC Purchase Agreement. Each issuance by Lotus Technology under the ELOC Purchase Agreement is subject to a maximum amount equal to 50% of the average daily trading volume in the ADSs on Nasdaq during the five trading days immediately preceding the day Westwood receives a valid purchase notice and subject to a US$100 million cap. The purchase price of ADSs that Lotus Technology may direct Westwood to purchase will be equal to 97% of the lowest daily volume-weighted average price during the three consecutive trading days beginning on the date that a purchase notice is delivered by Lotus Technology to Westwood. As consideration for Westwood’s irrevocable commitment to purchase ADSs upon the terms of and subject to satisfaction of the conditions set forth in the ELOC Purchase Agreement, Lotus Technology issued 700,000 Ordinary Shares to Westwood on September 19, 2024. In September 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. entered into a share sale and transfer agreement with Geely UK Limited, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Inc. sold 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH, or LTIC, a company incorporated in Germany and a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Inc., for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Business Updates 
summary: In 2024, our company achieved significant growth, delivering 12,065 vehicles, a remarkable increase of over 70% compared to the previous year, with China contributing around 25% of global deliveries. A convertible note purchase agreement was established with Geely HK on June 24, 2024, involving the acquisition of convertible notes totaling a specified amount in U.S. dollars, which Geely HK is raising through a private placement of RMB800 million. These notes, maturing on June 22, 2025, carry an interest rate of SORF plus 3.35% per annum, with conversion options into Ordinary Shares or American Depositary Shares (ADSs) starting 30 trading days post-issuance.

On September 16, 2024, we entered into an ELOC Purchase Agreement with Westwood Capital Group LLC, allowing for the purchase of up to $350 million in ADSs over 36 months, with specific purchase limitations and pricing conditions. Concurrently, we issued 700,000 Ordinary Shares to Westwood as part of this agreement. In September, we also sold 800,000 shares of our German subsidiary, Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH, to Geely UK Limited for EUR 10,880,000, resulting in Geely UK acquiring an 80% stake in LTIC.

On November 7, 2024, we established a bond subscription agreement with Kershaw Health Limited for a senior bond of $500 million, which is still pending finalization. Later that month, a share buyback agreement with Meritz was initiated, involving the repurchase of 17,500,000 ADSs linked to a previous business combination, structured to ensure a 12.5% internal rate of return for Meritz. This buyback is still under discussion.

In terms of leadership changes, Mr. Teik Huat Ooi resigned as director on December 6, 2024, and Mr. Abdul Farid Bin Alias was appointed effective January 22, 2025. Finally, on February 28, 2025, we entered into another convertible note purchase agreement with Geely HK for $70 million, with similar terms to previous agreements, including a maturity date of February 28, 2027, and a pledge of 50% equity interests in one of our subsidiaries.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Upon the Buyback Closing, certain provisions under the Meritz Subscription Agreement will terminate, including: (i) the obligation for Lotus Technology to maintain US$175 million of unrestricted cash as of the last date of each fiscal quarter; (ii) Lotus Technology's call option to acquire certain Meritz Shares at US$14.00 per share if the closing price of the Ordinary Shares for a five-trading-day period exceeds US$14.00; and (iii) Lotus Technology's obligation to deposit additional cash as collateral in the restricted cash account to secure its obligations due to changes in the closing price of the Ordinary Shares. The Buyback Closing has yet to occur as of the date of this prospectus and the Share Buyback Agreement provides for certain consequences in such event. Lotus Technology is in discussion with Meritz to further amend the agreements that have been entered into between the parties. On December 6, 2024, Mr. Teik Huat Ooi tendered his resignation as a director of Lotus Technology. The resignation was due to Mr. Ooi’s personal reasons and not due to any disagreement with Lotus Technology regarding its business, finance, accounting, and/or any other affairs. Lotus Technology appointed Mr. Abdul Farid Bin Alias as a director effective from January 22, 2025. On February 28, 2025, Lotus Technology Inc. entered into a convertible note purchase agreement, or the 2025 Geely CB Purchase Agreement, with Geely HK, pursuant to which Geely HK agrees to purchase from Lotus Technology Inc. convertible notes, or the 2025 Geely CBs, in an aggregate principal amount equal to US$70,000,000 through a private placement.

In terms of leadership changes, Mr. Teik Huat Ooi resigned as director on December 6, 2024, and Mr. Abdul Farid Bin Alias was appointed effective January 22, 2025. all_retrieved_content.page_content: The transaction is subject to customary closing conditions and the closing is expected to take place in the near future. The 2025 Geely CBs will mature on February 28, 2027, and bear an interest rate per annum equal to SORF on the issue date plus 3.35%, payable on the maturity date. Subject to the terms of the notes, the notes may be convertible into Ordinary Shares or ADSs at the option of Geely HK starting from the 30th trading day after the applicable issue date. The initial conversion price is equal to the volume-weighted average of the last reported sale price of the ADSs over the 10 consecutive trading days immediately preceding the applicable conversion date. In connection with the transaction, 50% equity interests of one of Lotus Technology Inc.'s subsidiaries will be pledged to Geely HK.
